text: The surgeon removes the inner stylet from the varus needle and feeds a loop into the groove of the loop passer. Using artery forceps, the loop is inserted into the varus needle until it extends into the peritoneal cavity, after which the loop passer is withdrawn.

The surgeon uses a grasper to pull extra length of the loop inside the peritoneal cavity. The tip of the appendix is held with the grasper and the loop is slid over it using the varus needle and grasper. The loop is tightened by pulling it outside, allowing manipulation of the appendix tip in various directions.

The surgeon works on the meso-appendix, using either a ligature or an extracorporeal knot for dissection. If the appendix is long, the loop around it is loosened to hold the appendix further proximally for easier handling and manipulation. Once the dissection is complete, the loop is removed and extracted from the varus needle.
